memberdef.argsstring: (this, nx, ny, nlevs, nsoil, nsoiltype, nsurftype, error_mgr, rc)
memberdef.name: metstate_init

detaileddescription.para: Initializes the meteorological state object, sets default values, and allocates required arrays.

parameterdescription.para: Number of grid points in x direction
Number of grid points in y direction
Error manager for context and error reporting

briefdescription.para: Allocate all arrays for MetStateType (optionally, only a specific field)

detaileddescription.para: Helper procedure to allocate and initialize arrays in the meteorological state.

parameterdescription.para: Name of the field to allocate (or 'ALL' for all fields)
Error manager for context and error reporting

briefdescription.para: Deallocate and clean up all arrays in MetStateType (field-by-field)

detaileddescription.para: Deallocates the specified allocatable array and resets scalar values in the meteorological state.

parameterdescription.para: Name of the field to deallocate (or 'ALL' for all fields)

briefdescription.para: Validate the MetStateType object for consistency and physical reasonableness.

detaileddescription.para: Checks that the number of levels is positive, temperatures and pressures are within physical ranges, and that required arrays are allocated.

parameterdescription.para: Error manager for context and error reporting

detaileddescription.para: Resets time, surface fields, and arrays to standard atmosphere values.

briefdescription.para: Check if required arrays are allocated in MetStateType.

detaileddescription.para: Returns .true. if all required arrays are allocated, .false. otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get approximate memory usage of MetStateType in bytes.

detaileddescription.para: Estimates the memory usage of all arrays in the meteorological state object.

briefdescription.para: Print a summary of the MetStateType object to standard output.

detaileddescription.para: Prints key fields, allocation status, and memory usage for diagnostics.

briefdescription.para: Allocate a specific field in MetStateType by name.

detaileddescription.para: Calls the generated select-case macro to allocate only the requested field.

parameterdescription.para: Name of the field to allocate

briefdescription.para: Deallocate a specific field in MetStateType by name.

detaileddescription.para: Calls the generated select-case macro to deallocate only the requested field.

parameterdescription.para: Name of the field to deallocate

briefdescription.para: Get a pointer to a vertical column for a given field name and (i,j) indices (type-safe)

detaileddescription.para: Calls the type-safe function version to obtain a real(fp) pointer, then assigns it to a polymorphic pointer for generic access. If the field is not found or not allocated, col_ptr is set to null and rc is set to CC_FAILURE.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the vertical column data (polymorphic pointer)
Return code (CC_SUCCESS if found, CC_FAILURE otherwise)

briefdescription.para: Get a scalar value from a 2D field at (i,j)
Get a scalar value from a scalar field.

memberdef.argsstring: (this, field_name, i, j, col_ptr, scalar_val, rc)
memberdef.name: metstate_get_field_ptr

briefdescription.para: High-level interface: get any field (column, 2D, or scalar)

memberdef.argsstring: (this, field_name, i, j, col_ptr, scalar_val, rc)
memberdef.name: metstate_get_field_ptr_int

memberdef.argsstring: (this, field_name, i, j, col_ptr, scalar_val, rc)
memberdef.name: metstate_get_field_ptr_logical

briefdescription.para: Get a pointer to a vertical column for a given field name and (i,j) indices (subroutine version)

detaileddescription.para: This subroutine version provides the interface expected by StateManager_Mod. It handles different variable types (2D fields, 3D fields, scalar values) and returns a 1D pointer to the appropriate data.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the vertical column data (1D array)
Return code (CC_SUCCESS if found, CC_FAILURE otherwise)

briefdescription.para: Derive meteorological fields from existing data.

detaileddescription.para: Calculates derived fields using existing meteorological variables. Supports common derived quantities like air density, virtual temperature, etc.

parameterdescription.para: Name of the field to derive
Error manager for context and error reporting
